title: xyris kibaraensis
intro: Xyris kibaraensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Xyridaceae, commonly known as the yellow-eyed grass. It is native to the high-altitude regions of Papua New Guinea. The medicinal properties of Xyris kibaraensis include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to reduce fever, soothe respiratory problems, and alleviate gastrointestinal issues, and it is often prepared as a tea or infusion for these purposes.
